name: Salt Sticks
recipeIngredient: 2 3/4 cups All-purpose flour, or as needed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, heat together the skim milk, lard or butter, water, salt, and 2 teaspoons sugar until the fat melts. Let cool to 110°F in a large bowl of electric mixer.
2. Meanwhile, combine warm water, pinch of sugar, and yeast in a separate bowl and let work until foamy, about 10 minutes.
3. Add the activated yeast mixture to the milk mixture.
4. Mix in a bowl the gluten flour, rye flour, and 1 3/4 cups All-purpose flour. Add 2 cups of the mixed flours to the yeast mixture and beat for 5 minutes on low speed.
5. Add remaining flour gradually, scraping the dough onto a floured board and kneading when the dough becomes too stiff to beat. Knead until very smooth, using additional All-purpose flour as needed to make a medium-stiff dough.
6. Form dough into a ball and turn into a buttered bowl. Turn buttered side up, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
7. Punch down the dough, turn onto a working surface, and divide into 4 pieces. Let pieces rest, covered with a towel for 5 minutes.
8. Roll each piece into an 10-inch round. Cut into 8 wedges, then roll each tightly from the wide end to the point, stretching and elongating the dough as you roll. Place on parchment-covered or lightly greased baking sheet (14 x 17) with the point of each roll underneath, leaving about 1 1/2 inches between salt sticks.
9. Continue rolling all dough until it is rolled out. You will fill 2 sheets.
10. Let sticks rise, covered, to proof for another 3/4 hour.
11. Preheat oven to 400°F. Brush with glaze and sprinkle with seeds and salt.
12. Bake on upper and lower racks of the oven for 5 minutes, then swap sheets and turn ends so browning is even. Bake for an additional 3 minutes, then reduce heat to 250°F and bake about 15 more minutes until evenly golden brown with no white spots on sides.
13. Cool on racks. May be frozen; to freshen, bake in a 350°F oven for 10 minutes.
